bull: 21% current earnings growth still exceeds the 14.1% priced into today's 42x multiple, per Trefis
RSI 36.8 and Stoch 7.8 show deep short-term oversold conditions after a -13% 20d slide
bear: Price is 27.9% below SMA200 and 15.2% below SMA50 — the downtrend is broad and not just a blip
Stock down ~40% YTD despite 'impressive' Q2 numbers, showing the market is repricing the growth multiple lower
ISRG.summary: Intuitive Surgical is being punished on valuation even as fundamentals stay strong, leaving it oversold but still in a confirmed downtrend.
ISRG.key_risk: Further multiple compression if revenue growth decelerates toward the 14% priced in, rather than the current 21%.

bull: RSI 28.5 is deeply oversold with the stock only 1.4% above its 52-week low, capping near-term downside
bear: A federal judge paused the $110B Warner Bros Discovery merger pending an antitrust trial that could run into next April
Deal uncertainty ('die of exhaustion') threatens the premium thesis and could drag on for months, per TheStreet
PSKY.summary: The Warner Bros Discovery merger — the core bull case — just hit a serious legal delay, adding a long, uncertain overhang.
PSKY.key_risk: Antitrust trial drags out or the merger collapses outright, erasing the deal premium embedded in the stock.

bull: Recurring subscription/asset-based-fee model (ETF-linked AUM growth) remains structurally intact per Zacks
Q2 revenue and cash flow actually beat estimates — the selloff is about the growth rate, not the business breaking
bear: Stock fell 10.1% in a single day on an earnings miss vs. elevated expectations — a sharp confidence shock
Price is now 5.8% below its own SMA50 after 3x average volume on the drop
MSCI.summary: MSCI's 'fine' Q2 print wasn't fine enough for a premium multiple, triggering a sharp single-day repricing that fundamentals don't yet justify undoing.
MSCI.key_risk: Further guidance cuts to subscription/ABF growth would validate the selloff rather than it being a one-off overreaction.

bull: EPS growth has outpaced the share price move, per Giverny Capital's Q2 letter — a valuation gap argument
Peer WRB just beat estimates on premium growth, suggesting sector-wide underwriting strength
bear: Progressive's own growth is described as 'slowing' in coverage of its excess-capital decision
5-day drop of -9.1% on 0.79x volume shows sellers without a clear one-off catalyst
PGR.summary: Progressive's earnings quality looks intact but growth deceleration and a capital-allocation debate are weighing on sentiment.
PGR.key_risk: Slowing premium growth compounds with margin normalization, undercutting the 'cheap relative to earnings' bull case.

bull: Q2 beat-and-raise with 5.4% organic growth and increased EPS guidance drove a 7.3% single-day pop on 3x volume
Price is now above both SMA50 (+9.5%) and SMA200 (+8.7%), confirming a fresh uptrend, not just a bounce
bear: RSI 63.9 and Bollinger position 113% (above the upper band) signal the move may be overextended near-term
3M's legacy litigation overhang (PFAS/earplugs) remains a structural risk unrelated to this quarter's beat
MMM.summary: 3M delivered a genuine beat-and-raise that flipped the technical picture bullish, though the size of the pop leaves it stretched short-term.
MMM.key_risk: A pullback/consolidation after the outsized one-day gain, or a re-emergence of legacy litigation costs.

bull: Memory chip shortage narrative (GM guidance, AI demand) drove a +14.3% single-day pop alongside Micron
Stock has more than doubled versus its SMA200, showing the underlying uptrend is still intact despite the recent pullback
bear: Extremely volatile — ATR of ~211 on a $1,589 stock and a -30% 20-day drop show the memory cycle can reverse violently
RSI 32.2 reflects real recent weakness, not just noise, ahead of any confirmation the shortage narrative sticks
SNDK.summary: Sandisk is a high-beta play on the memory shortage story — today's pop confirms the thesis is alive, but the swings are large in both directions.
SNDK.key_risk: A memory-pricing reversal or a supply response that undercuts the shortage narrative driving the recent rally.

bull: Analysts have been lifting earnings estimates into the print, and the stock jumped 7.5% on volume last week
RSI 39.3 leaves room to run if the upcoming earnings report confirms the improving estimate trend
bear: Stock is still 42% off its 52-week high and 17.2% below SMA200 — a large de-rating that hasn't reversed
Coverage explicitly flags 'uncertainty' heading into the print despite the rising estimates
ERIE.summary: Erie Indemnity has rising estimate momentum heading into earnings, but sits deep in a longer-term downtrend that a single beat may not fully repair.
ERIE.key_risk: An earnings miss after recent hype around rising estimates, reversing last week's volume-driven bounce.

bull: Analysts have been lifting earnings estimates for current and future fiscal years, per Zacks coverage
RSI 21.3 is deeply oversold, and the stock is still 85% above its 52-week low, showing large mean-reversion swings both ways
bear: Price is 23.8% below SMA50 and 21.4% below SMA200 — a steep, broad-based downtrend on lithium-price cyclicality
Coverage itself questions whether rising estimates reflect 'discipline or cyclicality' — i.e., may not be durable
ALB.summary: Albemarle is deeply oversold with improving estimate revisions, but the downtrend is steep and tied to a still-uncertain lithium price cycle.
ALB.key_risk: Lithium prices stay weak longer than the estimate-revision optimism assumes, extending the downtrend.

bull: RSI 19.3 and Stoch 0.2 are at extreme oversold levels, historically a zone prone to short-term bounces
bear: Stock is 53.8% off its 52-week high and 28.9% below SMA200, with consecutive down sessions in the latest coverage
Peer read-through (Lowe's 'discount with an asterisk') signals broader housing/home-improvement demand concerns
BLDR.summary: Builders FirstSource is in a severe, accelerating downtrend tied to housing-sector weakness that oversold readings alone haven't stopped.
BLDR.key_risk: Continued high mortgage rates or a housing slowdown extending the current downtrend further.

bull: The Clarity Act crypto market-structure bill clearing a major Senate/White House hurdle is a direct regulatory tailwind
Stock jumped 9.6-11% on the news with strong volume, and the broader 'AI trade' risk-on tone is supportive
bear: RSI 70 and Stoch 84 show the stock is now overbought after the pop
Still 20.4% below its own SMA200 and 58% off its 52-week high — the longer-term downtrend from the crypto pullback is intact
COIN.summary: Coinbase got a genuine regulatory catalyst in the Clarity Act progress, but the sharp pop has pushed it into overbought territory against a still-negative longer-term trend.
COIN.key_risk: A pullback from overbought levels or a stall/reversal in the Clarity Act's legislative progress.

bull: Stock is already down 80% from its 52-week high — much bad news on adtech growth deceleration looks priced in
As a scout candidate, a positive earnings surprise from this depressed base could trigger an outsized reversal
bear: Jefferies flags an 'uncompelling' setup into the print in either direction, capping conviction ahead of the catalyst
Price remains 40.5% below SMA200, and competitive pressure from Amazon/Google DSPs is a structural overhang
TTD.summary: Trade Desk is a battered adtech name heading into an earnings print that even bulls describe as unlikely to be a clean win either way.
TTD.key_risk: Another growth-deceleration miss on the imminent earnings report, extending the 76% YTD decline.

bull: Stock has stabilized recently (+3% over 5 days) after a brutal 70.8% drawdown from its 52-week high
Incoming CFO Robin Rossmann (an internal, long-time exec) could bring continuity and a renewed cost focus
bear: Sudden CFO departure (Christian Lown) injects leadership uncertainty right as the market debates fair value
Still 43.9% below SMA200 — no confirmed technical trend reversal yet, just a pause in the decline
CSGP.summary: CoStar is a deep-value scout candidate stabilizing after a steep decline, but an abrupt CFO transition adds fresh uncertainty before a trend change is confirmed.
CSGP.key_risk: The CFO transition proves disruptive or reveals financial/strategic issues that justify the prior de-rating.

bull: A broader payments M&A wave (Stripe reportedly eyeing PayPal) could extend to consolidation interest in Fiserv
20-day momentum is positive (+7.3%), a tentative sign of stabilization off the lows
bear: Still 23.6% below SMA200 and 69.8% off its 52-week high — no confirmed turnaround yet after last year's guidance issues
No company-specific catalyst in the news beyond sector-wide M&A chatter
FISV.summary: Fiserv shows early stabilization and sits in a sector where M&A activity is picking up, but the turnaround from its prior guidance-driven collapse is still unproven.
FISV.key_risk: The sector M&A narrative doesn't translate into any actual interest in Fiserv, leaving it without a re-rating catalyst.

bull: Broad semis rally today (Intel, KLA, Lam, Marvell, Micron all up) on dip-buying as the AI trade returns
RSI 23.9 was deeply oversold before today's 6.7% bounce, and price is still 58.8% above SMA200 on a longer view
bear: Still down 32.4% over 20 days — today's bounce hasn't undone a sharp recent slide
Custom AI silicon competitive landscape (in-house hyperscaler chips) remains a durable risk to Marvell's growth story
MRVL.summary: Marvell is bouncing hard with the broader AI/semis complex from oversold levels, though the 20-day trend remains sharply negative.
MRVL.key_risk: AI capex spending from hyperscalers slows or shifts further toward in-house silicon, pressuring Marvell's custom-chip revenue.

bull: Price sits above both SMA50 (+3.2%) and SMA200 (+1.9%), confirming an intact uptrend with a strong 3-year track record (+87.5%)
New Treasury infrastructure partnership and index/fintech push (Xtrackers migration) add incremental revenue diversification
bear: RSI 71.3 is overbought, and one analyst view puts the stock 7% above fair value
Market expects Nasdaq's beat streak to continue — a high bar that raises the risk of disappointment
NDAQ.summary: Nasdaq's uptrend and diversification initiatives are intact, but the stock looks stretched on both technicals and valuation heading into earnings.
NDAQ.key_risk: A rare earnings miss against the priced-in beat-streak expectation, triggering mean reversion from overbought levels.

bull: Long-term track record remains strong (175.6% total return over 5 years) and one Morningstar-style read flags it as below fair value
Price is well above SMA200 (+25.5%), showing the longer-term trend is still constructive
bear: Zacks explicitly says Nucor 'doesn't possess the right combination' for an earnings beat next week
Slightly below its own SMA50 (-2.3%) with soft 20-day momentum (-4.6%), suggesting near-term stalling
NUE.summary: Nucor's long-term uptrend and valuation case remain intact, but the model-based earnings read into next week's report is unfavorable.
NUE.key_risk: A steel-cycle-driven earnings miss next week that breaks the longer-term uptrend.

bull: Mizuho reiterated a bullish case as the stock recovers from its recent slide, tied to AI/cloud infrastructure demand
RSI 33.7 with a 4.7% bounce today suggests the oversold reversal may be starting
bear: Still 27.5% below SMA50 and 32.6% below SMA200 after a 62.8% drop from its 52-week high — a severe, unresolved downtrend
Oracle's OCI buildout is capital/debt intensive, making it highly sensitive to any slowdown in Big Tech AI capex (flagged via the Alphabet earnings read-through)
ORCL.summary: Oracle is attempting an oversold bounce on renewed AI-infrastructure optimism, but remains deep in a downtrend driven by concerns over debt-funded cloud capex.
ORCL.key_risk: Big Tech AI capex growth disappoints (as tested by upcoming Alphabet earnings), undermining the OCI demand thesis Oracle's valuation depends on.

bull: Morgan Stanley stays constructive on aerospace/defense ahead of Q2 prints, citing sector resilience
TDG's aftermarket-parts pricing power is a durable moat, and RSI 23.7 shows the stock is oversold
bear: Co-COO Joel Reiss just cut his stake by 46%, banking ~$3.2M in gains — a notable insider-selling signal
Analysts expect only single-digit earnings growth this quarter, a soft bar after a 22% stock decline over the past year
TDG.summary: TransDigm retains its aerospace aftermarket moat and sector tailwind, but recent insider selling and a modest earnings outlook temper the oversold bounce case.
TDG.key_risk: Insider selling proves to be a leading indicator of soft Q3 results or slowing aerospace aftermarket demand.
